title: The Wunder and Eva Unit 02s Second Mission help
display_title: The Wunder and Eva Unit 02s Second Mission help
wikitext:
[]: The Wunder and Eva Unit 02's Second Mission? help?             Quote Post by Rahxephon » Thu May 30, 2013 10:10 am What the hell exactly is going on during the second mission when the ships are on the water? The second mission that takes place right after they retrieve Shinji from space and tell him he won't be doing anything... I don't understand wtf it is that unit 02 is doing... What exactly is she doing with the super heated lance? what is she trying to reach for?? what are all those big tubes with rotating parts connected to what she is sticking the heated spear into?? Obviously they are part of the wunder because they start moving when people from the wunder start distributing power to it... so wtf is going on?? I understand they pull the "angel" (if thats even what it is) out of the water with the wunder and you see all those wierd hexagonic shapes fall out of the water, you can also see them when the wunder crew first goes into battle station one in the control room, and from below as unit 02 is using the heated spear to burrow towards it... so seriously... wtf is going on and why was that necessary for the wunder to take off and kill the angels? Also what are the giant columns of light that appear? I guess thats just how the angel attacks and vaporizes its targets and the main body and core block are what the wunder pulls out of the water and shoots? But if thats the case, then wtf are those Hexagonic shaped things that you see if not part of the angels attack or body?

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Thu May 30, 2013 10:26 am The "Angel" is Evangelion Mark.04 Code 4C (yes I know it doesn't look like an Eva). Read about it here: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/13585/-Capabilities-of-the-Nemesis-Series-Eva-Mark04/ The hexagonic shapes are apparently a "cloaking cocoon" it was concealed in, according to background dialogue. It was necessary for the Wunder to take off to drag the Code 4C out of the cloaking cocoon so it could destroy it, as well as to escape the "pillars of light" the Code 4C deploys (the weird red cross-shaped pillars of light). Asuka was using a "Heat Lance" to melt a hatch directly above Eva-01, so she could shove in an "igniter" which somehow made Eva-01 start producing energy for the Wunder. Read about it here: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/13653/The-Spears/80/? And discuss the mysteries of the Wunder here: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/13476/-AAA-Wunder/0/? All the information is available in the forum, it just needs to be found. Someone should probably lock this thread eventually.

[]:             Quote Post by Rahxephon » Thu May 30, 2013 10:36 am Well I apologize for being behind on the discussion I didn't get to watch movie 3 until a few weeks ago... ;( Thanks for the help anyway... But also, if that thing is an eva does it have a pilot? or is it operated by dummy system or something similar? thanks!

[]:             Quote Post by Charsi » Thu May 30, 2013 10:43 am Previously Wunder and her fleet have been running each time they get spotted by Nemesis series. Presumably a distraction of sorts is created, Evas are sortied, draw the attention and retreat, giving cover to the fleet. Misato basically decides to fight instead of run, even though the Wunder has never been activated nor flight tested. This runs against the suggestion of Ritsuko as well. Still, they pull it off and shoot big holes in the Nemesis series in the process. In order to activate Wunder, Asuka is tasked with cutting open it's belly. The belly comprises of layered armor, followed by a transparent hexagonal shielding covering some kind of organic matter or skin. Asuka cuts away the armor layers, grabs a giant tuning fork and sticks it through the skin of organic matter. This feeds power to Wunder, activating it and producing the purple light and sound. We're shown later that that organic skin or sac contains Eva-01 (we can see it's distinctive helmet numerous times). It is giving off so much power that merely piercing the skin of its containment (which is very similar to a womb) gives enough power to change Asuka's arm into core virtually instantly - she jettisons it hastily - power a massive battleship, and generate an AT field capable of propelling the Wunder, protecting it and it's crew, and holding an entire fleet of naval vessels aloft. Eva-01 makes a pretty sweet battery. ----------------------------------------------------------- To answer your follow up post: Wunder itself is (probably?) an Evangelion of sorts. The crew rises into something that resembles an entry plug, and undergoes a startup/activation/ionization process similar to that of an Evangelion. They don't "synchronize" so much as use consoles to command the ship. It's like the entire bridge crew is in one big entry plug. Eva-01 no longer "needs" a pilot and apparently gives off oodles of power regardless of Shinji's presence. One more answer: We don't know if the Nemesis series has pilots, but since there is a mention of Mark.06 being remodeled to an "autonomous type" it's not a big leap to imagine the Nemesis series don't require a human pilot either. It paves the way for a mass production series of autonomous Evangelions.

The belly comprises of layered armor, followed by a transparent hexagonal shielding covering some kind of organic matter or skin. Asuka cuts away the armor layers, grabs a giant tuning fork and sticks it through the skin of organic matter. This feeds power to Wunder, activating it and producing the purple light and sound. We're shown later that that organic skin or sac contains Eva-01 (we can see it's distinctive helmet numerous times). It is giving off so much power that merely piercing the skin of its containment (which is very similar to a womb) gives enough power to change Asuka's arm into core virtually instantly - she jettisons it hastily - power a massive battleship, and generate an AT field capable of propelling the Wunder, protecting it and it's crew, and holding an entire fleet of naval vessels aloft. Eva-01 makes a pretty sweet battery.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu May 30, 2013 11:03 am Rahxephon wrote: Only question I have now is if those Code 04A, B and C / Nemesis series have pilots, or dummy system or what.. that is if they are really Evas and not angels..

[]:             Quote Post by K40s » Thu May 30, 2013 11:49 am Reichu wrote: Kaworu says in-film that Mark.06 was modified into an "autonomous" unit, which might imply neither pilot nor dummy plug, but another mode of control entirely. "Autonomous" is probably what the Eva Mark.04s are.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Fri May 31, 2013 5:52 pm Speaking of which, why the hell were the ships floating?

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Fri May 31, 2013 11:02 pm Squigsquasher wrote: Speaking of which, why the hell were the ships floating?
